rationale: Ground truth is thin (mostly approval/off-topic comments) with one substantive inline ask about deepening reduce_scatter test verification; neither candidate directly surfaces that gap, though X's sweep at least engages with the reduce_scatter fake-group logic while Y never touches the test file's coverage question. X offers three grounded, file/line-anchored nits (stale comment, missing docstring, arguably-dead None check) with concrete fix instructions, giving it stronger actionability than Y's single vague 'worth a one-line comment' suggestion. Y's broader approve-with-verification narrative is well-grounded in vLLM API checks but yields almost no concrete, actionable findings.
